source_option.publication: Reduce the Department of Defense's Annual Budget
source_option.warning: CBO estimates the envelope but does not specify or assess a particular military alternative.

fiscal_path.basis: reductions from the then-current CBO projection of the cost of implementing DoD's plans
fiscal_path.outside_published_window: No savings are claimed for 2035 or 2036 until an independent scorer extends a specified force plan.
fiscal_path.emergency_and_war_funding: included in total national-defense cost and not an automatic escape from the voted path; any supplemental must identify funding or debt consequences on its ballot

implementation_choice.description: Preserve the 2025 composition by major unit type while reducing active-component personnel and associated units, support, operations, and acquisition toward a force approximately 17 percent smaller by 2034.
implementation_choice.phase_rule: DoD shall publish annual service, unit, end-strength, civilian, contractor, operations, maintenance, and acquisition schedules that fit the statutory fiscal path; it may shift no more than 5 percent of one year's required reduction among those categories without a public capability finding.
implementation_choice.prohibited_shortcut: No undisclosed across-the-board sequester and no reliance on unspecified administrative savings.

protected_obligations: earned military and civilian retirement benefits
veterans benefits and health care outside the Department of Defense budget
care for wounded, ill, and injured service members
nuclear command, control, and communications safety and continuity
pay and health benefits promised for service already performed

order: reduce accessions and use normal attrition
offer voluntary separation and retirement where skills allow
use involuntary separation only after the first two tools are documented as insufficient
protections: at least 180 days individual notice absent urgent operational necessity
credential translation and civilian placement assistance
two years of transition health coverage or an independently scored equivalent
pension and GI Bill rights already earned remain intact
formula-based community transition tied to verified employment and revenue loss, with beneficial-ownership disclosure

reviewers: Director of Operational Test and Evaluation
required_public_tests: force sufficiency against the current National Defense Strategy scenarios
readiness, deployment tempo, munitions, logistics, sealift, and industrial-base effects
personnel, contractor, installation, and community effects
capability_gate.failure_rule: A failed capability finding does not waive the test or create free spending. Before the binding ballot, the sponsor must publish a revised force schedule, substitute enumerated savings, or add enumerated revenue, and obtain a new joint score.
capability_gate.voter_disclosure: The ballot states every failed or disputed test and displays the fiscal replacement.

score_request: annual budget authority and outlays for 2027 through 2036 against a current baseline
annual service and active, reserve, civilian, and contractor workforce effects
unit, readiness, procurement, operations, maintenance, installation, and industrial-base effects
macroeconomic, interest, deficit, and debt effects
sensitivity to supplemental and emergency appropriations
